item.title: Much ado about marshmallows
item.description: Good news, it’s s’more VAT nonsense

item.title: ‘End of an era’ as ECB’s emergency pandemic stimulus unwinds
item.description: Nearly €3tn of excess liquidity will have drained from financial system by 2027, according to ECB estimates

item.title: The UK’s unpleasant energy exposure
item.description: The vulnerability stems from history and geography rather than failed policies

item.title: KPMG cleared by UK watchdog over audit of gambling group Entain
item.description: Decision is boost for Big Four firm seeking to repair reputation after series of scandals

item.title: Air France-KLM kicks off bidding race for Portugal’s TAP
item.description: Franco-Dutch group first to submit non-binding offer for up to 49.9% of carrier

item.title: Everything rises and BYD capitalises on oil crisis
item.description: The inside story on the Asia tech trends that matter, from Nikkei Asia and the Financial Times

item.title: Trump’s threats to pull out of Nato leave alliance exposed
item.description: Also in this newsletter: Greek fraud investigation zeroes in on government members

item.title: A peek into who owes what and to whom
item.description: Striking a chord

item.title: FirstFT: Trump threatens to hit Iran ‘extremely hard’
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: conflict’s business winners and Gulf pipelines

item.title: Vulture funds circle private equity
item.description: Distressed-debt funds see a potential bonanza in the downturn in private credit

item.title: World’s top energy traders wrongfooted in early days of Iran war
item.description: Oil traders that normally thrive on volatility caught out by scale of crisis as energy shock unfurled

item.title: From Lockheed to start-ups, arms makers jostle for Iran war orders
item.description: Middle East conflict holds out promise to bring another windfall for defence groups as governments restock arsenals

item.title: Gulf states consider new pipelines to avoid Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Conflict prompts countries to revisit plans that replicate Saudi Arabia’s East-West pipeline despite huge cost and complexity

item.title: Poolside hunts data centre partners after CoreWeave deal falls through
item.description: AI start-up held talks with Google and other cloud providers in bid to revive ambitious 2-gigawatt Texas project

item.title: Ofwat poised to waive Thames Water fines to 2030 under deal
item.description: Clock is ticking on approving creditors’ emergency offer for utility as October deadline looms

item.title: Car park collapse is a warning to leaseback-loving retailers
item.description: UK supermarkets that sell and then rent back their property should take note of NCP’s failure

item.title: The global wave of energy rationing
item.description: Governments from Bangladesh to Zambia impose measures to cut fuel demand as Middle East conflict cuts off flows

item.title: UK bank capital rules are becoming a drag on growth
item.description: Reforming the capital regime would unlock meaningful lending capacity and strengthen Britain’s competitiveness

item.title: NHS staff boycott Palantir’s data platform over ethical concerns
item.description: Controversial US tech group was awarded a £330mn contract in 2023 to collate hospital and patient information

item.title: Apple at 50: how Asia fuelled its rise to the top
item.description: Japanese industry and Chinese manufacturing prowess played roles in the tech giant’s success, with consequences for both

item.title: Muskism: A Guide for the Perplexed — and how tech turned to oligarchy
item.description: Quinn Slobodian and Ben Tarnoff pinpoint the SpaceX entrepreneur’s clever leveraging of state support to exert influence over government itself

item.title: US readies new pharmaceutical tariffs
item.description: Levies of 100% on certain medicines would implement threats Trump made last year

item.title: US Treasury calls in regulators for talks on private credit risks
item.description: Discussions will include American and international insurance watchdogs

item.title: Nasa’s first crewed mission to the Moon in more than 50 years blasts off
item.description: Artemis II overcomes technical delays to launch smoothly from Kennedy Space Center

item.title: Feud between Two Sigma founders continues to plague fund
item.description: Co-chief appointed to quell dispute quietly resigned last month citing ‘ongoing governance challenges’

item.title: Will $100 oil speed up the EV shift?
item.description: As fuel prices rise and the outlook becomes more uncertain, the economics of car choices and manufacturing become harder to ignore

item.title: Active social media use drops as Brits become more cautious online
item.description: Proportion who are actively posting, sharing or commenting has dropped since last year

item.title: Amazon in talks to buy $9bn satellite group Globalstar in bid to rival Musk’s Starlink
item.description: Ecommerce giant is trying to catch up with SpaceX’s low Earth orbit internet service

item.title: France probes Iran links in thwarted attack on Bank of America
item.description: Police arrest three men in possible connection with new Iranian group Ashab al-Yamin

item.title: SpaceX filing kicks off largest IPO process in history
item.description: Confidential submission to US regulator sets up summer listing
